title: Assignment 01
text:
# echo: true
# eval: true

import platform
import psutil

print("Operating System:", platform.system(), platform.release())
print("Python Version:", platform.python_version())
print("Machine:", platform.machine())
print("Processor:", platform.processor())

mem = psutil.virtual_memory()
print("Total Memory (MB):", round(mem.total / (1024**2), 2))
print("Available Memory (MB):", round(mem.available / (1024**2), 2))

Operating System: Linux 6.17.0-1010-azure
Python Version: 3.12.13
Machine: x86_64
Processor: x86_64
Total Memory (MB): 15989.71
Available Memory (MB): 14604.06

title: Projects
text:
Projects
Academic and personal projects in data analytics and machine learning




  
    
      
        PySpark
        SQL
        Python
      
      
        
        View on GitHub
      
    
    Spark SQL & Job Market Analysis
    Analyzed 70,000+ job postings using Spark SQL and DataFrames to uncover salary trends by job title, rank top companies offering remote roles in California, track monthly posting fluctuations, and compare compensation across major U.S. cities.
    
      
        4
        SQL queries
      
      
        70K+
        job postings
      
      
        5
        U.S. cities
      
    
    View live report →
  

  
    
      
        PySpark
        Plotly
        Big Data
      
      
        
        View on GitHub
      
    
    Big Data Visualization at Scale
    Built interactive visualizations across 72,000+ job postings using PySpark and Plotly — including box plots for salary by industry, bubble charts for occupation vs. median pay, scatter plots for experience vs. salary by education level, and histograms comparing remote, hybrid, and onsite compensation.
    
      
        72K+
        job postings
      
      
        5
        visualizations
      
      
        3
        work types
      
    
  

  
    
      
        Machine Learning
        PySpark
        Regression
      
      
        
        View on GitHub
      
    
    Salary Prediction with Regression Models
    Built and compared four machine learning models — Linear, Generalized Linear, Polynomial, and Random Forest Regression — to predict salaries from 32,000+ job postings. Minimum years of experience was the strongest predictor (72% feature importance in Random Forest), with the best model achieving an RMSE of ~$40,500.
    
      
        4
        ML models
      
      
        32K+
        records
      
      
        72%
        top feature importance
